table_semantics.justification: Full table structure with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and <th scope='col'> on every header cell. <time datetime> used for dates. Table is used only for tabular data with no layout abuse.

element_choice.justification: Native <input type='search'>, <select>, and <button> used throughout. <time datetime> used for all dates. Download CSV is a <button>. Previous/Next are <a> elements. No div-based interactivity.

form_semantics.justification: Every input and select has a visible <label> via wrapping label elements. Controls are grouped inside a <form> within a <search> landmark. Appropriate input type='search' used. No placeholder-only labels.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used sparingly and correctly: aria-current='page' on current pagination link, aria-disabled on the Previous link, aria-label on <search> and <nav>, aria-hidden on decorative chevron icons. No redundant roles on native elements. <section> has aria-labelledby.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> for the page title, followed by a visually-hidden <h2> for the section — correct two-level hierarchy with no skipped levels.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip links to both main content and search are present. <header>, <main>, <search>, <nav aria-label='Pagination'>, and <section aria-labelledby> all used appropriately. No landmark overuse.

content_realism.justification: Eight rows of realistic, diverse employee names, departments, roles, and dates. All three status values represented. 'Showing 8 of 42 employees' is plausible. No placeholder or lorem ipsum content.

list_semantics.justification: Pagination items are wrapped in a <ul> inside <nav>. No other list contexts are present in the design; no inappropriate use of lists.

expectations_met: Status column content is conveyed by text labels, not colour alone
Department and status filters use native <select> elements
Filter controls are grouped in both a <form> and a <search> element
Pagination current page indicated with aria-current='page'

'Showing 8 of 42 employees' is a

, not a heading
Table is not wrapped in role='grid'
No CSS display properties strip table semantics
additional_observations: Excellent use of <search> wrapping the <form> for the filter toolbar
<time datetime> used on all eight start dates — a detail many implementations omit
visually-hidden 'Status: ' prefix inside each badge ensures screen readers announce the status clearly
Two skip links provided: one to main content and one to the search/filter area
aria-disabled + tabindex='-1' on the disabled Previous link is a correct pattern for a static page context
